rows.col2: Yes — can diagnose and prescribe psychiatric medication

rows.col1: Behaviour, cognition, emotions, relationships, family dynamics

blocks.heading: When Do You Need Each Type?

children.text: A psychologist assesses psychological functioning, behaviour, and cognition. They're ideal when the court, school, or authority needs to understand:
How someone thinks, feels, and behaves
Ability to parent or make decisions
Impact of trauma, abuse, or neglect
Learning difficulties or educational needs
Whether someone can understand court proceedings (fitness to plead)
Personality, coping strategies, and risk factors
Common case types:
Family law (parenting capacity, attachment, child protection)
EHCP and school assessments (learning, behaviour, development)
Criminal law (fitness to plead, reliability of evidence, risk assessment)
Civil claims (psychological injury from accidents or negligence)
A psychiatrist provides medical diagnosis and psychiatric assessment. They're ideal when the case involves understanding:
Mental health diagnoses (depression, psychosis, personality disorders)
Effects of psychiatric medication
Whether a mental health condition affects capacity or functioning
Medical prognosis and treatment recommendations
Fitness to plead from a psychiatric perspective
Common case types:
Criminal law (fitness to plead, psychiatric assessment of dangerousness)
Family law (capacity of a parent with mental illness)
Immigration law (assessment of torture survivors, PTSD)
Civil claims (psychiatric injury, PTSD from assault or accident)
Many cases benefit from both a psychologist and a psychiatrist, because behaviour and medical diagnosis are intertwined:
A parent with untreated depression may lack motivation to parent effectively
A child's trauma may manifest in psychiatric symptoms (anxiety, PTSD)
Domestic abuse survivors show both psychological impact and psychiatric symptoms
Criminal defendants may have both psychological deficits and psychiatric illness
Cost-saving tip:
Instructing both experts is often more cost-effective than instructing one and then realising you need the other. Our Client Managers can advise whether one expert or two is optimal for your case.

blocks.heading: How We Recommend the Right Expert

caseStudies.title: Family Law Case: When Both Experts Helped
caseStudies.description: A care proceedings case involving a mother with bipolar disorder. Psychology assessed parenting capacity and family dynamics; psychiatry confirmed diagnosis, medication stability, and prognosis. The combination gave the court the complete picture.

caseStudies.title: Criminal Case: Fitness to Plead
caseStudies.description: A defendant with intellectual disability facing trial. Psychology assessed cognitive functioning; psychiatry assessed comorbid depression. Together, they provided evidence of fitness to plead.

caseStudies.title: Immigration: Torture Survivor
caseStudies.description: An asylum seeker claiming torture. Psychiatry diagnosed PTSD; psychology assessed psychological impact and rehabilitation needs. Dual assessment strengthened the claim.

faqs.question: What if I'm still not sure which expert I need?
faqs.answer: That's exactly what our Client Managers are for. Use the tool above to get a recommendation. Then call us on 01306 879 975. We'll discuss your case specifics and confirm whether you need psychology, psychiatry, or both — at no cost.

faqs.question: Can a psychologist do what a psychiatrist does?
faqs.answer: Not exactly. Psychologists cannot diagnose mental health conditions or prescribe medication. But they can assess psychological impact and functioning. If your case needs psychiatric diagnosis, you need a psychiatrist. If it needs behavioural or capacity assessment, a psychologist is sufficient.

faqs.question: Can a psychiatrist do what a psychologist does?
faqs.answer: Psychiatrists can assess psychological functioning to some extent. But they specialise in medical diagnosis. For cases focused purely on behaviour, parenting capacity, or learning difficulties, a psychologist is typically more appropriate — and often more cost-effective.

faqs.question: What if my case involves both psychology and psychiatry?
faqs.answer: Instruct both. Many complex cases — especially family law involving mental illness, or criminal cases with dual disability — benefit from dual assessment. It's often more cost-effective than instructing one expert and discovering mid-case you need the other.

faqs.question: How much more expensive is it to instruct both experts?
faqs.answer: Roughly 80–100% of the single-expert cost, not double. Both experts will review the same background materials and may coordinate their assessments, which is more efficient than two completely separate referrals.

faqs.question: How long does it take to find both a psychologist and a psychiatrist?
faqs.answer: We can provide CVs for both within 24 hours. Assessment timelines remain the same (4–6 weeks standard). Both experts can often assess within a similar timeframe.

faqs.question: Are psychiatrists more qualified than psychologists?
faqs.answer: They're differently qualified. Psychiatrists are medical doctors with specialist psychiatric training. Psychologists have doctoral-level psychology training. Each brings distinct expertise. 'Better qualified' depends on what the case needs, not seniority.

faqs.question: What if the expert I'm instructed declines the case?
faqs.answer: We have 1,000+ experts in our network across both disciplines. If the original expert declines, we immediately suggest alternatives — usually within hours, never longer than 24 hours.

blocks.heading: Still Want More Detail?

cards.title: Psychology Services
cards.description: We match HCPC-registered psychologists across all legal and educational specialisms. Use our tool to see if psychology is right for your case.

cards.title: Psychiatry Services
cards.description: We connect Section 12 approved psychiatrists for medical mental health assessment. Our tool recommends psychiatry when diagnosis is key.

cards.title: Dual Expert Matching
cards.description: Many cases need both. We'll match a psychologist-psychiatrist pair, coordinate their assessments, and manage everything.

cards.title: Educational Psychology
cards.description: Schools and LAs: Our Educational Psychologists assess learning, behaviour, and SEND. Use the tool to understand when EP input is needed.

blocks.heading: Know Your Expert Type? Let's Find Them.
blocks.bodyText: Once you know whether you need a psychologist, psychiatrist, or both, our Client Managers are ready to match you within 24 hours. We'll provide CVs, quotes, and expert alignment.
